commit.message:
feat: add /_null and /_router benchmark endpoints

Ultra-minimal diagnostic endpoints for isolating performance layers:

- /_null: bypasses entire BMW pipeline (no BmwContext, no middleware,
  no rate limiter, no auth). Writes 2-byte 'OK' via PipeWriter.
  Measures raw Kestrel + request handling ceiling.

- /_router: exercises EntityPrefixRouter.TryResolve on real entity
  slugs via the byte-span path. No WAL, storage, or rendering.
  Measures Kestrel + routing resolution cost.

Both endpoints:
- Zero allocations on hot path (ReadOnlySpan<byte> literals, GetSpan/Advance)
- Guarded by BMW_ENABLE_BENCH_ENDPOINTS=1 env var (disabled by default)
- Injected before Stopwatch/BmwContext creation in RequestHandler
- Static readonly bool lets JIT eliminate the branch in production

Usage: wrk -t8 -c400 -d30s --latency http://127.0.0.1:8080/_null

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: scan 'Munch Map' thread during import, no tag required

  bypassed so all messages with images are treated as potential pins;

Finalize block identity and time travel design decisions

BLOCK_IDENTITY.md:
- No opt-out (fundamental to Bloom)
- Eager first-run assignment (10K pages, self-write detection)
- Non-deterministic IDs (stored in file, not regenerated)
- Self-healing on history thread (indexer detects, never blocks)
- Cross-page move detection via persistent notification

TIME_TRAVEL.md:
- SPC H prefix for history (separate from SPC l links)
- Separate git repo in .index/.git/ (no conflict with user's git)
- Prefetch on which-key prefix press (SPC H = free compute time)
- LRU cache with predictive adjacency prefetch
- Reference vault: 10K pages, 10 years, ~8-10 MB git repo
- All interactive operations target < 10ms perceived latency

DAY_VIEW.md / JOURNAL_REDESIGN.md:
- Updated keybindings to SPC H c for calendar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Cosmos CI build optimizations (#48260)

* Optimize Cosmos CI: reduce PR matrix, increase parallelism, skip shade

1. PR-conditional emulator matrix (16 → 11 jobs):
   Drops redundant JDK variants for Spark/Kafka in PR builds.
   Full matrix on main merges.

   Dropped for PRs (5 jobs, ~5 agent hours saved):
   - Spark 3.3 Java 11 (keeping Java 8)
   - Spark 3.4 Java 8 (keeping Java 11)
   - Spark 3.5/Scala 2.12 Java 8 (keeping Java 17)
   - Spark 4.0/Scala 2.13 Java 17 (keeping Java 21)
   - Kafka Java 11 (keeping Java 17)

2. Increase BuildParallelization from 1 to 2 in all stages
   (Build, TestEmulator, TestVNextEmulator).

3. Skip maven-shade-plugin for non-Spark/non-Kafka emulator jobs:
   Core emulator, long emulator, and encryption jobs don't need
   Spark/Kafka uber JARs. Adding -Dshade.skip=true saves ~90s of
   shade plugin execution per Spark module × 5 modules = ~7-8 min
   per non-Spark job (5 jobs × 7 min = ~35 min agent time saved).

4. Remove outdated comment about emulator download time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: also skip maven-antrun-plugin when shade is skipped

The antrun 03-repack phase expects shade output (native .jnilib/.so
files in target/tmp/). When -Dshade.skip=true, the shade output doesn't
exist and antrun fails with 'Could not find file'. Add
-Dmaven.antrun.skip=true alongside -Dshade.skip=true.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: pass shade.skip to both build AND test steps via AdditionalArgs

The test step runs 'clean verify' which recompiles everything from
scratch, including Spark shade. Our BuildOptions only affected the
build step. Add -Dshade.skip=true -Dmaven.antrun.skip=true to
AdditionalArgs for non-Spark jobs so it flows into TestOptions too.

Keep BuildOptions for the build step as well (both steps need it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add BuildOptions plumbing to skip shade in Build stage unit test jobs

Add BuildOptions parameter through ci.yml → ci.tests.yml → build-and-test.yml
pipeline chain. Defaults to empty string (no behavior change for other SDKs).

Cosmos Build stage sets BuildOptions to '-Dshade.skip=true -Dmaven.antrun.skip=true'
to skip Spark/Kafka uber JAR creation during unit test matrix jobs, saving ~14 min
per job. The release artifact deploy step is unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add per-job ProjectListOverride for Spark/Kafka emulator jobs

Each Spark emulator job previously compiled ALL 14 modules including
other Spark versions it doesn't test, wasting ~11 min per job on
unnecessary shade+compile.

Changes:
- generate-project-list.ps1: Check for ProjectListOverride env var
  at the top. If set, use it directly and skip normal computation.
  Defaults to empty (no behavior change for other SDKs).
- Emulator matrix JSONs: Add ProjectListOverride for each Spark and
  Kafka job with only the modules they need (core + their specific
  Spark/Kafka module).

Example: Spark 3.5/2.13 job previously built 14 modules (41 min test
step). Now builds only 6 modules, saving ~11 min per Spark job.

Estimated savings: ~11 min × 9 Spark jobs + ~5 min × 2 Kafka jobs
= ~109 min agent time per full CI run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* No-op: trigger Cosmos CI for build optimization validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ProjectListOverride for emulator/encryption jobs to skip Spark compilation

Emulator, Long Emulator, and Encryption jobs were compiling all 14 cosmos
modules including 7 Spark modules (Scala compilation ~10-16 min) despite
only running core emulator tests. Add ProjectListOverride to limit these
jobs to only the modules they actually test:
- Emulator/Long Emulator: azure-cosmos, azure-cosmos-test, azure-cosmos-tests
- Encryption: adds azure-cosmos-encryption

Also reverts the no-op TestSuiteBase trigger commit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard ProjectListOverride from being used in FromSource runs

FromSource runs use ClientFromSourcePom.xml to build all track 2 libraries
and rely on the computed project list for -pl/-amd test scoping. Allowing
ProjectListOverride to take effect during FromSource runs would restrict
the test scope incorrectly. Check $env:TESTFROMSOURCE and skip the
override when it is 'true'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: port SF engine core — network patterns, protocols, guards, discussion

Major rewrite of the Rust SF engine to match the Python platform:

Engine (engine.rs):
- Network pattern: multi-round agent discussion (vision + review phases)
- run_intake(): Jarvis + RTE + PO discuss user requests before acting
- Phase gates: [VETO]/[APPROVE]/[NOGO] detection between phases
- Context budget: 6K chars max, truncation for context rot

Protocols (protocols.rs — NEW):

commit.message:
Persist session cookie and auto-show login dialog on expiry (#11)

* Persist session cookie and auto-show login dialog on expiry

- couchGetSession() now returns a discriminated SessionStatus union
  ('authenticated' | 'expired' | 'offline') so network errors no longer
  incorrectly trigger a login dialog when the device is offline
- checkSession() in auth store leaves isAuthenticated unchanged on 'offline',
  only clears it on confirmed 'expired'; returns SessionStatus for callers
- Added invalidateSession() to auth store for sync-layer use
- On startup, auto-show login dialog when session is confirmed expired
  (not when offline — app stays usable with local IndexedDB data)
- Visibility change handler re-checks session when PWA is foregrounded
  after being backgrounded overnight, showing login dialog if expired
- Keep-alive: pings /_session every 5 min while authenticated to refresh
  the CouchDB AuthSession cookie during active foreground use
- watch(isAuthenticated) now shows login dialog when session transitions
  from true→false during active use (e.g. 401 detected during PouchDB sync)
- Sync error/denied handlers detect 401/403 and call invalidateSession()
  instead of scheduling a retry

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MHGzG2eLdnQ6gsAn6VweFV

.NET SDK exposes DefaultValue but the Web API does not surface it.
Removed the field entirely — verified live: HTTP 204 success.

Integer column defaults must be set post-creation via the Maker
Portal or solution XML import.

repository.description: This repository contains a fully unconditional and spectral proof of the Riemann Hypothesis using S-finite adelic systems, without invoking ζ(s) or the Euler product. The canonical operator D(s) ≡ Ξ(s) is constructed geometrically and validated via explicit formulas, Fredholm determinants, and Paley–Wiener uniqueness. Includes formalization in Lean

commit.message:
ci: raise benchmark alert threshold to 120% during baseline stabilization

With only 3 baseline data points on shared CI runners, 115% is too tight —
BM_SessionManager_CreateMany/5 triggered a false positive at exactly 1.15x.
Session creation benchmarks allocate ~2MB ring buffers per session, making
them sensitive to memory allocator variability on shared runners.

Raise to 120% until we have 10+ data points, then tighten back to 115%.

commit.message:
Add language labels to code blocks

Override the 'pre' renderer in MarkdownBlock to wrap each code block
in a .code-block-wrapper div with a .code-block-lang label top-right.

Language detection reads the language id from the rehype AST node's
className (e.g. 'language-python') — always reliable since it comes
directly from the fenced code block marker in the model output.

A 70+ entry LANG_LABELS map normalises ids to display names:
  py/python → Python, js/jsx → JavaScript, ts/tsx → TypeScript,
  cpp/c++ → C++, cs/csharp → C#, rs/rust → Rust, go/golang → Go,
  sh/bash/shell → Bash, ps1/powershell → PowerShell, sol → Solidity,
  yaml/yml → YAML, dockerfile → Dockerfile, and many more.
Unknown ids fall through as-is; plaintext/text ids are hidden.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix loopback bypass for IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses

When Kestrel listens on [::] (dual-stack), IPv4 connections arrive as
::ffff:127.0.0.1 which IPAddress.IsLoopback doesn't recognize. Use
MapToIPv4() to unwrap the mapped address before checking.

commit.message:
fix: harden mmap code against races, use-after-dispose, and edge cases

MappedSegment:
- Replace TOCTOU volatile _disposed check with reader-count gate
  using Interlocked CAS — Dispose spins until in-flight reads drain
  before releasing the mapping (fixes C1: use-after-dispose race)
- Guard totalBytesU > int.MaxValue before cast (fixes H5: truncation)

MappedSegmentCache:
- Use TryRemove in Dispose loop to prevent double-dispose with
  concurrent Evict calls (fixes H1: double-dispose race)

MappedPagedFile:
- Use Interlocked.Exchange for atomic single-dispose (fixes H3)
- Validate pageIndex >= 0 and offset >= 0 to prevent negative pointer
  arithmetic reading before the mapped region (fixes M4: segfault)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: modal overlay now properly covers entire screen including sidebar

- Dialog overlay: z-50 → z-[500], opacity bg-black/50 → bg-black/60
- Dialog content: z-50 → z-[501] (above overlay)
- Sheet overlay/content: same z-index bump for consistency
- Chatbot: z-[99] → z-[200] (below dialog overlay but above page content)
- Dashboard layout: added 'relative' so Chatbot absolute pos anchors correctly

Root cause: Chatbot was z-[99] > Dialog overlay z-50, making it pierce the
modal backdrop. Sidebar appeared unmasked for the same reason.

commit.message:
fix: prevent EXC_BREAKPOINT crash from stale file watcher references (#661)

Fixes a use-after-free crash (EXC_BREAKPOINT / SIGTRAP) that occurred
when file watcher callbacks fired after the associated WebContents had
been destroyed. Four related bugs are addressed:

1. Add `webContentsId` to `WatchEntry` so watches can be associated with
a specific window/WebContents.

2. Implement `cleanupWatchesForWindow` — previously a no-op stub that
iterated watches without stopping any. Now stops all watches owned by
the closing window.

3. Guard `sender.isDestroyed()` in the debounce callback. If the sender
is gone, the watch is proactively stopped instead of silently swallowing
send errors while the watcher keeps running indefinitely.

4. Wire an `AbortController` to the `READ_TREE` IPC handler so
long-running directory traversals are cancelled when the initiating
WebContents is destroyed, preventing late-resolving
`FileHandle::CloseReq` callbacks from firing into an invalid V8 context.

Also calls `cleanupWatchesForWindow` on the window `close` event and
`stopAllWatches` in the `before-quit` handler to ensure all watchers are
torn down during the app lifetime.

Adds a new test suite for `file-watch-service` and expands
`file-handlers.test.ts` to cover the AbortSignal wiring.

Closes #528

---------
